name: Saute of Squid, Shrimp and Eggplant
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel eggplants and cut into 1/4-inch-thick slices lengthwise. Sprinkle with salt on both sides and chill for 1 hour to allow bitter flavors to leach out.
2. Cut into 1/4-inch strips, reserving 4 whole slices. Cut body of squid into 1/2-inch diagonal slices. Keep tentacle section whole. Reserve.
3. Shell and devein shrimp. Cut peppers into 1/8-inch strips. Peel and seed tomatoes and chop coarsely.
4. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a skillet until hot. Add the reserved 4 whole slices of eggplant and cook until golden brown on both sides, but do not overcook. Remove from heat and keep warm.
5. Reduce heat and add pepper strips and garlic, adding more oil as needed. Saute for 1 minute.
6. Add squid, reserved tentacles, shrimp, and eggplant strips to the skillet. Cook until shrimp turn pink.
7. Add chili powder and tomatoes. Continue to simmer gently.
8. Add salt and lemon juice to taste. Remove from heat.
9. Place a slice of eggplant on each plate. Spoon the shrimp mixture over the eggplant.
